headline: 2 primary contracts identified: proxy + implementation; control roles include owner, supplyController, and assetProtectionRole
short_headline: 2 contracts on Ethereum; PAXG proxy with admin controls

findings.text: Etherscan name-tag search for 'Paxos Gold' on Ethereum returned proxy at 0x45804880De22913dAFE09f4980848ECE6EcbAf78 with implementation at 0x74271f2282ed7ee35c166122a60c9830354be42a
GitHub repository README documents proxy address 0x45804880De22913dAFE09f4980848ECE6EcbAf78 as the canonical interaction point for PAXG token
GitHub repository lists audit reports by ChainSecurity and CertiK; addresses examined for in-scope contracts
GitHub source code repository paxosglobal/paxos-gold-contract contains contract documentation and deployment information

Contract roles identified: owner (pause control), supplyController (mint/burn), assetProtectionRole (freeze/wipe), feeController, feeRecipient, betaDelegateWhitelister; using AdminUpgradeabilityProxy pattern with proxy_admin role for upgrades

rationale.verdict: Discovery phase: catalogued 2 primary smart contracts on Ethereum mainnet (proxy + implementation) with associated administrative roles (owner, supplyController, assetProtectionRole, feeController, feeRecipient). No specific admin addresses recovered from on-chain reads in this run due to tool constraints; next assessment with read API can pin actual signers. GitHub and Etherscan document the proxy architecture and role structure.

evidence.shows: PAXG token proxy contract verified on Etherscan; shows proxy pattern with implementation at 0x74271f2282ed7ee35c166122a60c9830354be42a

PAXGImplementation contract; contains owner, supplyController, assetProtectionRole, feeController, feeRecipient, betaDelegateWhitelister state variables; AdminUpgradeabilityProxy pattern from ZeppelinOS

GitHub repository README confirms proxy at 0x45804880De22913dAFE09f4980848ECE6EcbAf78 as canonical interaction point; documents supplyController, owner (pause), assetProtectionRole, feeController roles; references ChainSecurity and CertiK audits
Official website confirms PAXG as gold-backed ERC-20 token; redirects to docs, audits, and deployment information

unknowns: D1-offchain: Could not execute read calls to determine actual owner, supplyController, assetProtectionRole, feeController, or feeRecipient addresses in this run; addresses are stored in proxy contract storage and require on-chain reads via /api/contract/read or direct state inspection
D5: Protocol configured with single chain (Ethereum); no multi-chain enumeration required
D6: No factory contracts identified in initial discovery; PAXG appears to be a single ERC-20 token rather than factory-deployed pool system

D8: Admin addresses discovered in this run are limited to contract creation info (deployer) from GitHub and Etherscan metadata; actual role holders (owner, supplyController, etc.) stored in contract state and flagged for next assessment

protocol_metadata.about: Paxos Gold (PAXG) is an ERC-20 token backed by physical London Good Delivery gold bars stored in LBMA-approved vaults. Each PAXG token represents one troy ounce of allocated gold held in custody by Paxos Trust Company, an OCC-regulated institution. The token can be redeemed for physical gold bullion or fiat currency, offering investors fractional ownership of regulated, audited gold with blockchain settlement finality.
